findings.title: Untrusted local handoff can bypass BOS-confirmed pull-request readiness
findings.description: A handoff with status confirmed is accepted solely from workspace JSON. validate_confirmed_readiness/1 only checks that receipt is a map and that caller-controlled pullRequest fields match the stored identity. It does not validate a durable BOS receipt or re-fetch the PR through GameApi.Client. A preexisting matching confirmed file therefore skips the readiness coordinator and permits the merge-capable deployment-verifier role to start without affirmative proof that bos_mark_pull_request_ready ran or that the PR remains open, non-draft, and exact-HEAD.
findings.recommendation: Treat the handoff as intent/cache only. Before advancing, confirm the operation receipt and current PR state through GameApi.Client/bos-mcp using the immutable operationId, runId, PR number, and expected HEAD. Add tests that fail for fabricated confirmed JSON and demonstrate response-loss recovery from durable BOS state rather than an in-memory fake.

summary: Changes requested: exact-commit tests pass, but the security boundary relies on agent-writable workspace JSON rather than authoritative BOS confirmation, allowing readiness enforcement to be bypassed before the merge-capable role starts.
